codeReview.summary: The extraction is broadly implemented (new SectionCard + migrated call sites + Vitest coverage), but there are UI-parity regressions in the migrated surfaces that violate the feature’s expected behavior.

issues.description: SummaryStats now renders the summary glyph twice: once in `title` (lines 134-139) and again via SectionCard `icon` (lines 144-148). The pre-refactor header had a single icon, so this is a visible parity regression.

SectionCard hardcodes neutral title/description colors (`text-gray-*`) regardless of tone (lines 71-72). In ResultsStep read-only mode (`tone="info"`), this drops the previous sky-emphasis typography (previously `text-sky-*`), so the extracted card does not preserve UI parity.
Header-only cards now pass an empty `<div />` child (also at line 95) to satisfy `children`, which forces SectionCard’s `mt-5` body wrapper and introduces avoidable empty spacing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker procedure and mission AGENTS require explicit Red→Green TDD, but this session’s transcript skeleton shows the new test file being added before any targeted failing test run; the first targeted SectionCard test command appears only after implementation and is green.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId be386202-7aa2-4fd4-851c-2dd1ea46dff2 lists ApplyPatch calls creating SectionCard/SectionCard.test.tsx before `cd frontend && npm test -- --run SectionCard` (exitCode 0), while handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.

summary: Failing review: the feature lands the extraction and tests, but introduces visible UI-parity regressions (duplicate SummaryStats icon and loss of info-tone title/description styling in read-only snapshot cards).
